metadata.title: Ambitious and Determined: For Young Professionals

chunks.title: Heart-Brain Balance: Emotional Intelligence
chunks.content:
Heart-Brain Balance: Emotional Intelligence

**Pathway ID:** `621666f8-4a72-419e-8d70-57b5fb6d1bfa`

**Title:** Heart-Brain Balance: Emotional Intelligence

**Duration:** 10:54:41  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 14 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

The technical skills that helped secure your first promotion can’t guarantee the next one. If you want to be in a leadership position, there is an emotional aspect that you need to remember. It's what makes you effectively mentor teams, handle tension, provide feedback, and connect with others. It's called emotional intelligence, accounting for almost 90% of what sets top performers apart from rivals with equivalent technical abilities and expertise.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: A Plan for Everything: Organizational Strategies
chunks.content:
A Plan for Everything: Organizational Strategies

**Pathway ID:** `483368be-231c-4efd-817e-acc93b44080c`

**Title:** A Plan for Everything: Organizational Strategies

**Duration:** 2:58:35  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 2

**Description:**

You've possibly heard that nine out of ten companies have failed to execute their plans. So, the obvious question is why? The response to that is complicated. Strategies struggle for hundreds of reasons: some are under-researched, others do not include the right individuals, and some actually do not track the right components. But you should be in the 10% company that really sticks to a strategy plan—and we can help you with that with this learning path.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

metadata.title: A Plan for Everything: Organizational Strategies

chunks.title: Critical Skills for Critical Times
chunks.content:
Critical Skills for Critical Times

**Pathway ID:** `e19cb84b-c051-4549-b9d9-e4dc85bff9f8`

**Title:** Critical Skills for Critical Times

**Duration:** 6:09:22  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 9 | **Books:** 2

**Description:**

Critical times demand critical skills… We live in a time of depression and anxiety, tough days of financial hardship, health issues, emotional loneliness, social disconnection, and terror. The challenges, both personally and professionally, have made our lives tough, making it possible to slip into the pit of creating a negative mindset. The fact is, there are many reasons to feel pessimistic about the prospects for the near future, but negative mindset will do little but trigger more despair. And in view of the difficulties ahead, only a positive mindset and the adoption of new skills will lead us to a satisfactory life. we will help you adopt them in this learning path...

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Leadership & Influence
chunks.content:
Leadership & Influence

**Pathway ID:** `aa89a1b4-6f68-431e-9ccc-d15f7d4fa50e`

**Title:** Leadership & Influence

**Duration:** 2:52:22  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 4 | **Books:** 0

**Description:**

This learning path sounds pretty maniacal from the title, right? Ok, the search for insight on "how to get people to do what you want" doesn't have the strongest connotation. But in fact, the art of getting people to agree with you and get "on your side" is an invaluable skill in life, particularly in business. Not to mention, the ability to motivate and guide others to accomplish a shared purpose is an important trait in leadership. One of favorite quotes is: “Leadership is the art of getting someone else to do something you want done because he wants to do it. Being able to inspire people to take specific action is an art – and it's completely interesting to learn about it! in this learning path, you will find some of the best ways to get people to do what you want them to do.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Become an Influential Business Writer
chunks.content:
Become an Influential Business Writer

**Pathway ID:** `216194a9-be6e-466d-934b-77d07d77c991`

**Title:** Become an Influential Business Writer

**Duration:** 3:08:29  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 0

**Description:**

Want to raise your profile in your industry? Or establish yourself as an expert in your niche? According to influencers and successful leaders, if you want to be a great leader you need to be a great writer. In today’s world, writing plays a pivotal role regardless of your expertise and skills.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Rule with Communication Skill
chunks.content:
Rule with Communication Skill

**Pathway ID:** `1a5c1e49-1ea3-494c-a0a8-28bb0c9e09f7`

**Title:** Rule with Communication Skill

**Duration:** 5:07:31  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 6 | **Books:** 5

**Description:**

Effective communication is about more than just exchanging information. It’s about understanding the emotion and intentions behind the information. As well as being able to clearly convey a message, you need to also listen in a way that gains the full meaning of what’s being said and makes the other person feel heard and understood. Learn how to clearly convey information and ideas in an engaging manner. Explore how to build trust, communicate effectively, and be heard in all parts of your organization. Learn important interpersonal communication skills and strategies for igniting curiosity, attention, trust, and respect.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Building Trust
chunks.content:
Building Trust

**Pathway ID:** `26d7754e-75fe-4355-bb8b-b63306e6c0ca`

**Title:** Building Trust

**Duration:** 2:52:00  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 3 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

What's at the heart of a work environment? One word, trust. It is completely important to build not only your credibility, but also a broad network of people who will support you in your career. It goes without saying: no one is responsible for the success of a company; it's a collective effort. "There is a closely connected sequence of events that has to happen in every organization to produce success. Follow the tips in this learning path to establish trust in your colleagues, clients, and your supervisors."

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Traction: Get a Grip on Your Business
chunks.content:
Traction: Get a Grip on Your Business

**Pathway ID:** `e85aefd3-2c78-4689-ba29-a5322800fb3c`

**Title:** Traction: Get a Grip on Your Business

**Duration:** 1:28:50  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 1 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

Nearly every failed startup has a product. What failing startups don't have are enough clients. Customer traction is crucial to success. There should be a good business plan for a startup to thrive. And to come up with a good business plan, you should have paying clients. Customer Momentum is very critical for the startup process. This means how many paying or non-paying consumers there are who are using/subscribing to the product or service and how the time-related amount is increasing. Here is your customer traction learning path.

**Learning Path Content:**

Getting Past No: Negotiation Skills

**Courses:** 4 | **Books:** 2

**Description:**

Everyone wishes to get to Yes, so what happens if someone keeps saying No to you? How would you successfully deal with a stubborn manager, an angry client, or a deceitful coworker? This learning path provides a tested breakthrough processes to turn opponents into negotiating partners. With state-of-the-art negotiating techniques built for everyone.

chunks.content:
Ongoing Improvement

**Pathway ID:** `83036fa6-c36b-479a-8988-a1010e04c5a9`

**Duration:** 6:12:05  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 7 | **Books:** 2

**Description:**

Businesses are constantly looking at opportunities to improve their practice. The history of business is rich in stories of firms that were once at the zenith of popularity only to find themselves usurped by hungry competition. But is it possible to organize the company's improvement, in other words, will this method of change prove fruitful and worthwhile? Yes, yes. There are a variety of approaches that have been created to effectively search out and act on opportunities to help grow the market and bring an end to competition. and you will find them in this learning path.

**Learning Path Content:**

chunks.content:
Miss the Mess: Improve Your Organizational Skills

**Duration:** TBD  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0

**Description:**

Companies look for quality workers that have strong organizational qualities that tend to be more appealing when rooted in the everyday routine. You can develop these skills by learning and self-discipline. When you develop organizational skills, you can start applying them on a daily basis to form a lifelong habit and experience greater success at work. In this learning path, we describe and include examples of core organizational skills like time management and office filing and so on.

**Learning Path Content:**

chunks.title: Covert Persuasion
chunks.content:
Covert Persuasion

**Pathway ID:** `1b29f715-df43-47a8-b24c-ee796db14ad9`

**Title:** Covert Persuasion

**Duration:** 3:51:02  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 4 | **Books:** 3

**Description:**

Are you a comfortable influencer in your office, or do you feel restricted by your position or your personality? Imagine you're in charge of the marketing department. However, you are unable to reassure the people a transition is a good one. Some of them are resistant, although others embrace only grudgingly. Their morale seems to be at an all-time low, so you know that expressing your authority is not the solution. You're in damage control mode. This is your chance to learn some new styles of influence and keep the team on the ground. Take this learning path to help you raise your influence strengths, using a variety of methods and techniques that can be applied in various scenarios for different individuals.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Self Starters and Inner Leaders
chunks.content:
Self Starters and Inner Leaders

**Pathway ID:** `572c2cba-89aa-4cb7-9aa6-5b53cd04b38a`

**Title:** Self Starters and Inner Leaders

**Duration:** 1:46:24  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 3 | **Books:** 0

**Description:**

Self-starter means that you are considered to be a leader and a proactive person. You're not asking for concrete arrangements or encouragement to follow your major goals—you're acting. Your go-getter mentality means that you want to get things done, but you get irritated because you have to wait on other people. You remember, that's the time you should be taking initiative, and the stalled progress means that your attention and resources are diminishing. Take the initiative and enroll in this learning path to adopt the proactive mindset.

**Learning Path Content:**

1. **Course**: Lead Yourself
2. **Course**: Proactivity
3. **Course**: Accountability


**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Productivity: Smarter Not Harder
chunks.content:
Productivity: Smarter Not Harder

**Pathway ID:** `614ff51f-bcb8-4d23-a529-2399217354c1`

**Title:** Productivity: Smarter Not Harder

**Duration:** 10:15:53  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 12 | **Books:** 10

**Description:**

Manage time more effectively and work more efficiently with practical strategies and options. Essential, but often overlooked, skills to help you be more organized, work efficiently, increase productivity and save time. Improving time management is a core skill for anyone wishing to work efficiently and establish good habits. Implementing your action plan will help you reduce stress and enhance your productivity.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Effective Professional Collaboration
chunks.content:
Effective Professional Collaboration

**Pathway ID:** `a594598e-80f2-40c7-8eba-d4ce8015bf83`

**Title:** Effective Professional Collaboration

**Duration:** 4:33:52  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

Just like in sports, working with others in your job makes you far more effective than when you work alone. Larger tasks and projects can’t be accomplished by just one person and, usually, better ideas come about when multiple people put their heads together. Collaboration is key to improving output, quality, and effectiveness. Each person brings something unique to the table and contributes to achieving the best possible result. Luckily, thanks to technology, there are now even more ways to work together efficiently.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Mastering Professional Communication
chunks.content:
Mastering Professional Communication

**Pathway ID:** `b5bee982-e3f6-4f83-81bc-97f94cf42ee6`

**Title:** Mastering Professional Communication

**Duration:** 5:14:55  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 7 | **Books:** 2

**Description:**

Effective and professional communication skills are essential to success in today's workplace. Learn the tools for effective verbal and written communication that will enable you to communicate with clarity and impact. Gain the skills required to effectively manage conflict and navigate difficult conversations and develop mindful listening and communication techniques. By increasing your professional communication skills, you minimize the potential for misunderstandings and ambiguities and increase your ability to inspire through clear and focused communication strategies.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Work Under Pressure
chunks.content:
Work Under Pressure

**Pathway ID:** `e5ab27fb-9ca8-4676-bd94-4a1c00b5efad`

**Title:** Work Under Pressure

**Duration:** 5:38:46  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 8

**Description:**

Employers will often ask if you can work well under pressure. It’s a sought-after skill many organizations value highly. If you possess work under pressure skills, you can maintain level-headedness when urgent needs arise instead of getting stressed out and overwhelmed. No matter where you work, there will undoubtedly be occasions where you have to work in high-pressure situations. In many cases, the difference between an average employee and an excellent one is the ability to perform well in a crisis situation when a quick decision is needed.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Adapting to Change
chunks.content:
Adapting to Change

**Pathway ID:** `92b68843-0fea-4270-b5e7-6d04615fe9cf`

**Title:** Adapting to Change

**Duration:** 4:47:59  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 7

**Description:**

While many find it hard to admit, we are predominantly creatures of habit. We prefer the known to the unknown, the familiar to the unfamiliar. Unfortunately, the rate of change continues to accelerate and there is little that we can do to avoid it. If we try to resist it, we are going to be left behind – in our careers and personal lives. The good news is that there are tangible skills we can learn to adapt to this change, which once learned can help others to master them as well.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Become a Senior Manager
chunks.content:
Become a Senior Manager

**Pathway ID:** `b244d065-5667-4b6d-9869-cfc3037cf30c`

**Title:** Become a Senior Manager

**Duration:** 13:21:44  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 10 | **Books:** 16

**Description:**

The best way to understand the “how” of a successful transition is to understand “what” is involved. Trying to succeed in a new domain or level of action doesn’t work if we rely on mindset of the prior level. What are the skills and responsibilities of the senior manager that differ from those of the manager? What is your role? All of these answers are addressed in this learning path, which is designed to help you transitioning to a senior manager, where you learn how to lead with the speed of trust, managing teams, team building, coaching, influence and persuasion skills. Also, you will learn how to manage key stakeholders, managing crisis, how to present to c-level managers, and other skills to help you being extraordinary manger.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Building Personal Power Through Influence
chunks.content:
Building Personal Power Through Influence

**Pathway ID:** `1aed75f9-f400-42a9-9581-307d5f498e8b`

**Title:** Building Personal Power Through Influence

**Duration:** 15:52:26  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 17 | **Books:** 10

**Description:**

Getting things done in the business world requires more than simply issuing orders. And most of us don't have the titles or authority to issue them in the first place. Along with being credible and building trust, you get results without direct authority by using persuasion and influence. Effective persuasion methods and influencing skills are the difference makers for getting results, with or without positional authority. This learning path will help you engage and enlist others in getting things done without the resort of formal power. It explores persuasion techniques and ways to promote influence through effective communication, reciprocity, the exchange of professional 'currencies', and persevering through resistance.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Information Technology Basics
chunks.content:
Information Technology Basics

**Pathway ID:** `bdef4ff0-148a-4d91-b1e0-168cbb93cac5`

**Title:** Information Technology Basics

**Duration:** 5:03:34  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 15 | **Books:** 0

**Description:**

Information Technology (IT) refers to the use of computer technology to manage information. The field of IT encompasses all computer software, hardware and related devices employed in the processing, transfer, storage, and dissemination of data whether it’s on a computer, smartphone, TV or other medium. Every time you download a song, stream a movie, check your email or perform a web search, you’re accessing IT services. Areas of study that fall within IT include database development, computer networking, software engineering, data analysis and more.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Etiquette in Business World
chunks.content:
Etiquette in Business World

**Pathway ID:** `1a76f7d1-46b3-4bfd-bb22-b23f22dae3bf`

**Title:** Etiquette in Business World

**Duration:** 6:03:36  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 8 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

As times change, so do social norms for personal and professional behavior, but that doesn't mean basic etiquette doesn't matter. Performance and quality are important, too, of course, but not exclusively. We sometimes forget that business is about people. There is no shortage of competent and reliable people in the business world and manners can make the difference. Wouldn't you rather collaborate with, work for or buy from someone who has high standards of professional behavior?

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Journey to the Socio-Behavioral Skills
chunks.content:
Journey to the Socio-Behavioral Skills

**Pathway ID:** `07211c0b-58c2-4331-9d8d-9d920d3183a6`

**Title:** Journey to the Socio-Behavioral Skills

**Duration:** 16:11:28  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 17 | **Books:** 12

**Description:**

Never wondered how achievement is not linked to technical knowledge only, but also to the way you acquire and apply your knowledge — the 'how'. It's the behaviors and skills needed to acquire and implement effectively, which fall under the umbrella of 'socio-behavioral skills'. In this Learning Path, you will get to know more about how socio-behavioral skills are linked to developing a learning mindset that boosts your work performance.

**Learning Path Content:**



**Metadata:**


---

chunks.title: Leaders of Tomorrow
chunks.content:
Leaders of Tomorrow

**Pathway ID:** `f4b0e3bd-31d4-4e37-b960-ee7c81d97412`

**Title:** Leaders of Tomorrow

**Duration:** 4:28:28  
**Average Rating:** 0.0/5.0  
**Courses:** 5 | **Books:** 4

**Description:**

Leadership is all about reaching goals and creating new leaders by developing your team skills. In this learning path, you will get to know the essential skills needed for you as a leader to develop your team, create new leaders, and reach your goals. You will also learn about team dynamics, leadership styles, creativity development, and negotiation skills.

**Learning Path Content:**
